name: Toffee Coated Popcorn
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Peanuts; roasted or mixed nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Put popcorn and nuts in a 6-quart bowl and stir.
2. In a microwave-safe glass measuring cup, combine the sugars, butter, water, and salt.
3. Cover with wax paper and microwave on high for 2 to 4 minutes, until steaming hot and butter has melted.
4. Stir with an oiled wooden spoon until the sugar crystals are almost completely dissolved.
5. Cover again and microwave on High for 2 to 4 minutes, until the mixture comes to a rapid boil.
6. Remove the wax paper and attach a microwave-safe candy thermometer to the side of the casserole, making sure the bulb is submerged in the toffee mix.
7. Microwave, uncovered on High for 8 to 10 minutes, until the temperature reaches 311°F (very hard-crack stage).
8. Immediately pour the hot toffee mixture over the popcorn and nuts. Stir with an oiled wooden spoon until evenly coated.
9. Spread the toffee-coated popcorn onto a marble slab or two large baking sheets. Using the spatula, flatten into a single layer.
10. Allow to cool for 10 to 15 minutes, or until firm.
11. Using your fingers, break the candied popcorn into bite-sized pieces.
12. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.
